html:
“This armor is durable but heavy, and slows its wearer.”

-

The Marble Suit is a wearable Armor, crafted in the Armor Filter. It requires 6 marble and 2 Ropes to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.

It has 735 durability and absorbs 95% of physical damage, and will also reduce the player's speed by 30% when equipped. Not considering its durability, the marble armor effectively adds 2850 hp to the average survivor.

Skins

Main article: Belongings

  • The Woven - Elegant Marble Plate Mail and Marble Splint Armor skins were introduced during the official The Forge event in 2017.
  • The Loyal Marble Impact Armor skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from July 31, 2025 to August 28, 2025.
Woven - Elegant
Forge Collection
Marble Plate Armor
This armor is the fastest way there is to get chiseled abs.
See ingame
Woven - Elegant
Forge Collection
Marble Splint Mail
This splint mail is crafted of many marble slabs chained together.
See ingame
Woven - Elegant
Winged Victory Collection
Marble Victory Armor
This armor may be heavy, but it makes your warrior's spirit soar.
Loyal
Orbital Collection
Marble Impact Armor
Make your mark in steel and style.

Tips

  • Because of this armor's speed penalty, it is better suited for tanking damage rather than kiting an opponent. This makes it best on mighty Wolfgang who, thanks to his damage bonus, can kill enemies quickly reducing the damage the armor takes and denying the movement speed penalty when Mighty
  • This armor's high protection makes it a good tanking armor on characters with low max health, such as Maxwell, allowing them to heal less often when tanking a boss. This is especially true in Don't Starve Together where armor absorption does not stack.
  • Due to the speed penalty, it isn't practical to wear this armor all the time. Players can switch to it when they want to tank in combat. Alternatively, a Walking Cane can be equipped along with the Marble Suit, negating most of the speed penalty. However, Walking Cane is not a decent weapon for most encounters. Experienced players can mostly overcome this problem by switching to a weapon before they attack, and switching back to Walking Cane before they dodge. Or alternatively, you can take off the suit when trying to avoid an attack and put it back on after you avoid an attack, but this is a fairly risky move which can require practice.
  • The decreased durability can leave players deterred from the chestplate. If the player desires to save on Marble while still regularly using this powerful chestplate, wearing armor that takes up the head slot is the best course of action. Football Helmets have 80% protection. By wearing the two pieces of armor, the Marble Suit will only absorb 51.6% of all incoming damage (and 54.3% of all damage absorbed by armor). Doing so, the relatively small 735 durability is effectively increased to 1,353 - almost double.
  • Wolfgang is unaffected by the speed penalty of this item while in his Mighty form. This makes it an ideal armor due to its high protection with virtually no drawback.

 Trivia

  • The Marble Suit was added in the The End Is Nigh update.
  • The Marble Suit is renewable, since Marble can be obtained from Earthquakes in Caves ot be mined from Marble Shrubs grown from Marble Beans.
  • A character wearing a Marble Suit makes a small clanking noise when walking.
  • Before the May 28, 2020 patch for Don't Starve Together, the cost of the Marble Suit was double its current cost, taking 12 marble and 4 rope.

"armormarble"
“This looks really heavy.”
–Wilson
“If I have to fight I might as well be protected.”
–Willow
“I tie rocks to muscles.”
–Wolfgang
“Could stop the daggers that stab at mine heart.”
–Wendy
“EXTRA HEAVY DUTY CASING.”
–WX-78
“An interesting choice of materials.”
–Wickerbottom
“It's hard to swing an axe while wearing this.”
–Woodie
“That really isn't my style.”
–Maxwell
“Its weight increases when worn, but not when carried. Fascinating!”
–Wagstaff
“Nearly impenetrable!”
–Wigfrid
“Marbelous protection!”
–Webber
“That's just overkill.”
–Walani
“Weighs a ton.”
–Warly
“This'd sink like a stone in th'sea.”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS ARMOR MADE OF ROCK BITS”
–Wilba
“Heavy”
–Wormwood
“It's like wearing a tank. Like driving a tank too.”
–Wheeler
“Protects your inner workings.”
–Winona
“Stone cold!”
–Wortox
“It heavyyyy...”
–Wurt
“Will I be able to walk in that?”
–Walter
“Well it's definitely sturdy, but why does it have to be so heavy?!”
–Wanda
“This armor is durable but heavy, and slows its wearer.”
-
The Marble Suit is a wearable Armor, crafted in the Armor Filter. It requires 6 marble and 2 Ropes to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.
It has 735 durability and absorbs 95% of physical damage, and will also reduce the player's speed by 30% when equipped. Not considering its durability, the marble armor effectively adds 2850 hp to the average survivor.
Skins[edit | edit source]
Main article: Belongings
The Woven - Elegant Marble Plate Mail and Marble Splint Armor skins were introduced during the official The Forge event in 2017.
The Loyal Marble Impact Armor skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from July 31, 2025 to August 28, 2025.
Woven - Elegant
Forge Collection

This armor is the fastest way there is to get chiseled abs.

Tips[edit | edit source]
Because of this armor's speed penalty, it is better suited for tanking damage rather than kiting an opponent. This makes it best on mighty Wolfgang who, thanks to his damage bonus, can kill enemies quickly reducing the damage the armor takes and denying the movement speed penalty when Mighty
This armor's high protection makes it a good tanking armor on characters with low max health, such as Maxwell, allowing them to heal less often when tanking a boss. This is especially true in Don't Starve Together where armor absorption does not stack.
Due to the speed penalty, it isn't practical to wear this armor all the time. Players can switch to it when they want to tank in combat. Alternatively, a Walking Cane can be equipped along with the Marble Suit, negating most of the speed penalty. However, Walking Cane is not a decent weapon for most encounters. Experienced players can mostly overcome this problem by switching to a weapon before they attack, and switching back to Walking Cane before they dodge. Or alternatively, you can take off the suit when trying to avoid an attack and put it back on after you avoid an attack, but this is a fairly risky move which can require practice.
The decreased durability can leave players deterred from the chestplate. If the player desires to save on Marble while still regularly using this powerful chestplate, wearing armor that takes up the head slot is the best course of action. Football Helmets have 80% protection. By wearing the two pieces of armor, the Marble Suit will only absorb 51.6% of all incoming damage (and 54.3% of all damage absorbed by armor). Doing so, the relatively small 735 durability is effectively increased to 1,353 - almost double.
Wolfgang is unaffected by the speed penalty of this item while in his Mighty form. This makes it an ideal armor due to its high protection with virtually no drawback.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Marble Suit was added in the The End Is Nigh update.
The Marble Suit is renewable, since Marble can be obtained from Earthquakes in Caves ot be mined from Marble Shrubs grown from Marble Beans.
A character wearing a Marble Suit makes a small clanking noise when walking.
Before the May 28, 2020 patch for Don't Starve Together, the cost of the Marble Suit was double its current cost, taking 12 marble and 4 rope.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Bộ Đồ Cẩm Thạch là một Bộ Giáp có thể đeo được, được chế tạo trong Bộ Lọc Giáp. Nó cần 6 viên bi và 2 Dây thừng để chế tạo và Máy luyện kim để tạo nguyên mẫu.

== {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips== * Because of this armor's speed penalty, it is better suited for tanking damage rather than kiting an opponent. This makes it best on mighty [[Wolfgang]] who, thanks to his damage bonus, can kill enemies quickly reducing the damage the armor takes and denying the movement speed penalty when Mighty * This armor's high protection makes it a good tanking armor on characters with low max health, such as [[Maxwell/Character|Maxwell]], allowing them to heal less often when tanking a boss. This is especially true in ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'' where armor absorption does not stack. * Due to the speed penalty, it isn't practical to wear this armor all the time. Players can switch to it when they want to tank in combat. Alternatively, a [[Walking Cane]] can be equipped along with the Marble Suit, negating most of the speed penalty. However, Walking Cane is not a decent weapon for most encounters. Experienced players can mostly overcome this problem by switching to a weapon before they attack, and switching back to Walking Cane before they dodge. Or alternatively, you can take off the suit when trying to avoid an attack and put it back on after you avoid an attack, but this is a fairly risky move which can require practice. * The decreased durability can leave players deterred from the chestplate. If the player desires to save on Marble while still regularly using this powerful chestplate, wearing armor that takes up the head slot is the best course of action. [[Football Helmet]]s have 80% protection. By wearing the two pieces of armor, the Marble Suit will only absorb 51.6% of all incoming damage (and 54.3% of all damage absorbed by armor). Doing so, the relatively small 735 durability is effectively increased to 1,353 - almost double. * [[Wolfgang]] is unaffected by the speed penalty of this item while in his Mighty form. This makes it an ideal armor due to its high protection with virtually no drawback. == {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * The Marble Suit was added in the ''[[Version History#April 9, 2013 - The End is Nigh|The End Is Nigh]]'' update. * The Marble Suit is renewable, since Marble can be obtained from [[Earthquakes]] in [[Caves]] ot be mined from Marble Shrubs grown from [[Marble Bean]]s. * A character wearing a Marble Suit makes a small clanking noise when walking. * Before the May 28, 2020 patch for ''Don't Starve Together'', the cost of the Marble Suit was double its current cost, taking 12 marble and 4 rope. == {{Pic32|Research Notes}} Assets == Marble Suit= Marble Suit Dropped.png|Dropped Marble Suit Equipped.png|Equipped |-| Marble Plate Armor= Marble Plate Armor Dropped.png|Dropped Marble Plate Armor Equipped.png|Equipped |-| Marble Splint Mail= Marble Splint Mail Dropped.png|Dropped Marble Splint Mail Equipped.png|Equipped |-| Marble Victory Armor= Marble Victory Armor Dropped.png|Dropped Marble Victory Armor Equipped.png|Equipped |-| Marble Impact Armor= Marble Impact Armor Dropped.png|Dropped Marble Impact Armor Equipped.png|Equipped == {{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == Marble Suit Wilson.png|[[Wilson]] wearing a Marble Suit.
